Just as I promised Musa's mother (AS) that I would return her baby to her, and I fulfilled that promise, your city is like your mother. I will bring you back to your mother city. Don't grieve over leaving.

قُل رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ - "Say: My Master knows best who has come with guidance." They've driven you out of the land, claiming you're misguided, that you're insane. But Allah ﷻ tells you to respond: "My Master knows better."

وَمَنْ هُوَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ - "And who is clearly misguided." Time will reveal who was truly on guidance and who was lost in clear misguidance.

translation: And you were not expecting that the Book would be conveyed to you, but [it is] a mercy from your Lord. So do not be an assistant to the disbelievers
commentary:
وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُوٓا۟ أَن يُلْقَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُوٓا۟ أَن يُلْقَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ - "And you were not expecting that the Book would be conveyed to you." The Prophet ﷺ wasn't sitting around hoping for revelation. He wasn't aspiring to prophethood. Just as Musa (AS) wasn't expecting to meet with Allah ﷻ on top of a remote mountain in the middle of nowhere, the Prophet ﷺ wasn't anticipating this tremendous responsibility.

إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ - "Except as a mercy from your Master." This entire burden you carry, this message you've been entrusted with, it came purely as a mercy from Allah ﷻ. Not because you sought it, but because He chose you for it.

فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ - "So don't be a supporter for those who disbelieve." The word ظَهِير means a backup, someone who supports from behind. In this context, it's as though Allah ﷻ is telling the Prophet ﷺ: you've turned your back on them by leaving, and now don't think, "But those are my people, I should go back." No. Don't back them up anymore. Don't take a soft corner toward them after they've rejected the truth.

Once migration happens, a door closes. The invitation to those who drove out the Messenger ﷺ becomes sealed. This is the weight of rejection when it reaches its ultimate conclusion.

translation: And never let them avert you from the verses of Allah after they have been revealed to you. And invite [people] to your Lord. And never be of those who associate others with Allah
commentary:
وَلَا يَصُدُّنَّكَ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أُنزِلَتْ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ

وَلَا يَصُدُّنَّكَ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أُنزِلَتْ إِلَيْكَ - "And let nothing prevent you from the signs of Allah ﷻ after they have been revealed to you." The verb صَدَّ means to block, to obstruct, to turn someone away from something. Don't let anything stop you, don't let anything obstruct you from proclaiming and living by the ayat of Allah ﷻ now that they've been sent down to you.

وَٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ - "And call to your Master." Continue the mission. Invite people to Allah ﷻ. Your role hasn't ended; it's entering a new phase.

وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ - "And never be among those who associate partners with Allah ﷻ." In this context, it carries an additional meaning beyond the theological: don't be in their company anymore. Don't remain in their ranks. Don't stay in Makkah among those who commit shirk. The physical and spiritual separation is now complete.

translation: And do not invoke with Allah another deity. There is no deity except Him. Everything will be destroyed except His Face. His is the judgement, and to Him you will be returned
commentary:
وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ ۘ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُۥ ۚ لَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ

وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ - "And do not call upon, with Allah ﷻ, any other deity." The verb دَعَا encompasses both calling upon in worship and calling upon for help. Don't worship anyone alongside Allah ﷻ. Don't seek aid from anyone as you seek it from Him.

لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ - "There is no deity except Him." This is the fundamental testimony of faith, the declaration that anchors everything. In a surah that began with Firaun's claim to divinity, that showed us Qarun's self-worship through materialism, we return to the pure statement of Tawheed: there is no god except Allah ﷻ alone.

كُلُّ شَىْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُۥ - "Everything will perish except His Face." Every single thing, كُلُّ شَىْء - everything that exists, هَالِكٌ - is perishing, is in a state of destruction, is temporary. Qarun's wealth perished. His mansion was swallowed by the earth. Firaun's kingdom perished. Empires rise and fall. Civilizations come and go. Everything in creation is temporary except وَجْهَهُ - His Face, His Essence, His Being.

لَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ - "To Him belongs all judgment," all wisdom, all rule, all authority. وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ - "And to Him you will all be returned." Every single one of you, every human being who has ever lived, will return to Him. We came from Him, and to Him we return.

This concluding ayah pulls together all the threads of the surah. The materialism of Qarun, the tyranny of Firaun, the struggles of Musa (AS), the journey of the Prophet ﷺ - all of it points to the same truth: everything perishes except Allah ﷻ. To Him alone belongs judgment, and to Him we will all return. Whatever authority anyone claims in this world, whatever wealth they accumulate, whatever power they wield - all of it is temporary. The only permanent reality is Allah ﷻ, and the only wise choice is to live in recognition of that truth.
